en.commentary:
The Bambu Lab P2S Combo takes the top spot, and the gap to second place is wider than the price difference suggests. Bambu solved the three problems that defined consumer 3D printing for a decade in a single $799 package: bed leveling is now invisible, multi-color is now plug-and-play through AMS 2 Pro with active filament drying, and the new PMSM servo extruder delivers roughly 70% more grip than the P1S, which translates directly into reliable engineering-filament prints that previously required X1-class hardware. After a month of mixed PLA, PETG, and ASA workloads, my P2S has never failed a multi-day print. That sentence would have been impossible to write about any sub-$1,000 printer in 2024.

The X1-Carbon Combo holds second place at $1,449, and the right buyer is the small print farm or the engineer running carbon fiber and PA-CF daily. The lidar first-layer scan and AI failure detection are genuinely useful at scale, the hardened steel hotend is the only correct answer for abrasive composites, and the chamber temperature monitoring matters for engineering thermoplastics. For a hobbyist printing PLA and PETG, the X1C is overspec — the P2S delivers 90% of the result for 55% of the price.

Prusa CORE One at $1,199 assembled is the answer for the open-source loyalist and the long-horizon owner. The all-steel exoskeleton, active 55°C chamber, load-cell first-layer calibration, and lifetime firmware updates make this the printer that will still work in 2034. Prusa's support is the gold standard of consumer hardware, and the upgrade path from MK4S means existing Prusa owners can preserve their investment. The trade-off is multi-color: the MMU system is more fiddly than Bambu's AMS, and that gap is the reason CORE One sits at third rather than first.

The Bambu P1S at $399 is the highest-value purchase in the entire category, and I recommend it to anyone testing whether they actually want to 3D print. It is the same enclosed CoreXY architecture as the P2S, runs the same Bambu software stack, accepts the same AMS 2 Pro for upgrade later, and costs less than half. The trade-off is the older stepper extruder and a louder fan profile — both real but not deal-breakers for hobby use. If you're new and unsure, buy this. If you find yourself printing daily after three months, the P2S Combo upgrade is the right next step.

The Elegoo Centauri Carbon at $319 deserves the seventh position despite being the lowest-priced enclosed CoreXY in the entire market because price alone doesn't tell the story. Enclosed CoreXY at this price was impossible in 2024. The Centauri Carbon delivers genuinely usable speeds, vibration compensation, and a Klipper-based firmware that runs circles over Marlin-era budget machines. The reliability gap to Bambu is real and the software ecosystem is thinner, but for a maker who wants to learn the mechanics rather than trust a black box, this is the entry point that didn't exist a year ago.

highlights.title: The Bambu P2S Combo is the right answer for 90% of buyers in 2026
highlights.body: At $799 the P2S Combo bundles the new PMSM servo extruder, AMS 2 Pro four-color drying, 500 mm/s print speeds, and full Bambu ecosystem integration. The servo extruder alone delivers 70% more grip than the P1S, which fixes the under-extrusion problems that haunted engineering filaments at speed. After a month of daily mixed-material printing my unit has not failed a single multi-day job. The P2S Combo sits in the price-performance sweet spot of the entire consumer category, and the gap to the X1C feels narrower with every firmware update.

highlights.title: The X1-Carbon Combo is overspec for hobbyists, perfect for print farms
highlights.body: The X1C costs $1,449 against the P2S Combo's $799, and the right way to evaluate that gap is to ask what daily workload justifies it. If you run PA-CF, PEEK, or PA-GF every week, the hardened steel hotend and chamber temperature monitoring are non-negotiable. If you run a print farm, the lidar first-layer scan and AI failure detection pay back in scrap reduction within months. If you print PLA models for a hobby, the X1C is bringing a chassis dyno to a Sunday drive. Buy the P2S, save the $650, spend it on filament.

highlights.title: Prusa CORE One is the only printer I trust to still work in 2034
highlights.body: Bambu's hardware reliability is excellent today, but Bambu's software model is a walled garden with mandatory cloud account and proprietary slicer. Prusa CORE One ships with open firmware, PrusaSlicer is the most respected consumer slicer in the category, and Prusa's spare parts catalog covers machines from 2012. The all-steel exoskeleton and active 55°C chamber make this a machine that prints engineering filaments at production quality, and the MK4S upgrade path means existing Prusa owners can preserve their tooling. At $1,199 assembled or $799 as a kit, this is the long-horizon answer.

highlights.title: The Elegoo Centauri Carbon at $319 broke the budget enclosed CoreXY barrier
highlights.body: An enclosed CoreXY printer for $319 was impossible 18 months ago. The Centauri Carbon delivers all-metal hotend, vibration compensation, Klipper-based firmware, and an enclosed chamber at a price that previously bought only open-frame bed slingers with marginal speeds. The reliability gap to Bambu is real, the software ecosystem is thinner, and the build quality reflects the price point. For a learner who wants to understand the mechanics or a maker on a strict budget, this is the entry that finally bridges the gap between $200 toys and $700 serious machines.

highlights.title: The Ender 3 V3 SE at $218 is still the right first printer for true beginners
highlights.body: Before recommending a $799 P2S to a newcomer, I always ask whether they've assembled a 3D printer or watched one fail mid-print at 2 AM. The Ender 3 V3 SE is the right way to learn what 3D printing actually is — a bed slinger with CR Touch auto-leveling and a Sprite direct extruder at $218 lets you make mistakes, swap nozzles, rebuild the hotend, and understand why higher-end CoreXY machines cost what they cost. Three months in, you either know you love this hobby and upgrade to the P2S, or you sell the SE for half price and lose $109. That's the lowest-risk path into the category.

zh-tw.commentary:
Bambu Lab P2S Combo 拿下第一名，跟第二名的差距其實比價差還大。Bambu 用一台台幣兩萬六的機器，一次解決了消費級 3D 印表機過去十年三大痛點：自動調平變成隱形的背景任務，多色列印透過 AMS 2 Pro 即插即用還能主動烘乾線材，新的 PMSM 伺服馬達擠出器抓力比 P1S 大 70%，這個改變直接反映在工程塑料的列印穩定度上，過去要 X1C 等級才能跑的材料現在 P2S 就能搞定。

我這個月混 PLA、PETG、ASA 連續操機，P2S 沒有一次多日列印失敗，這句話在 2024 年沒有任何一台 1000 美金以下的機器配說。直接講，這台就是 2026 年中階消費級的新標竿。

X1-Carbon Combo 約台幣四萬八排第二，會買這台的人應該是小型 print farm 或天天跑碳纖、PA-CF 工程塑料的工程師。雷達首層掃描跟 AI 失敗偵測在量產規模下確實有用，硬化鋼噴頭是耐磨複合材料的唯一正解，腔體溫度監測對工程熱塑料來說很重要。

但如果你只是業餘玩家印 PLA 跟 PETG，X1C 真的太超規。P2S 用 55% 的價格給你 90% 的結果，多出來的台幣兩萬一拿去買線材比較實在。

Prusa CORE One 約台幣三萬九組裝完成版排第三，是開源派死忠跟長期擁有派的答案。全鋼外骨骼、主動腔體加熱 55 度、load cell 首層校正、終身韌體更新，這台到 2034 年都還會繼續運作。Prusa 的客服是消費級硬體的金本位，從 2012 年的舊機型零件都還買得到。MK4S 老用戶可以用升級套件改裝，老投資不會浪費掉。

要妥協的是多色功能，MMU 系統比 Bambu AMS 麻煩很多，這個差距就是 CORE One 沒辦法搶到第一的關鍵原因。

Bambu P1S 約台幣一萬三是整個類別 CP 值最高的選擇，我推薦給所有還在猶豫要不要玩 3D 列印的人。封閉式 CoreXY 架構跟 P2S 一樣，跑同一套 Bambu 軟體生態系，可以後續加購 AMS 2 Pro 升級多色，但價格只要一半。要妥協的是舊款步進馬達擠出器跟風扇比較吵，這兩點是真的但對玩具用途來說不是 deal breaker。新手不確定要不要入坑就買這台，三個月後天天在用就升級 P2S Combo。

Elegoo Centauri Carbon 約台幣一萬出頭排第七，雖然是全市場最便宜的封閉式 CoreXY，但單看價格不能說完整故事。這個價位的封閉式 CoreXY 在 2024 年根本不存在。Centauri Carbon 給你堪用的速度、振動補償、Klipper 韌體，把 Marlin 時代的廉價機甩在後面。

跟 Bambu 的可靠度差距是真的，軟體生態也比較單薄，但 maker 想學底層機構而非信任黑盒子的話，這台是去年根本不存在的入門。

highlights.title: Bambu P2S Combo 是 2026 年九成買家的正確答案
highlights.body: 約台幣兩萬六的 P2S Combo 包進新的 PMSM 伺服馬達擠出器、AMS 2 Pro 四色乾燥系統、500 mm/s 列印速度、完整 Bambu 生態系整合。光是伺服馬達擠出器就比 P1S 抓力大 70%，這個改變直接修掉高速列印工程塑料時的出料不穩問題。我這個月每天混料列印一次失敗都沒有，這個價位給你這個結果，整個消費級類別的 CP 值甜蜜點就在這裡。每次韌體更新跟 X1C 的差距都在縮小。

highlights.title: X1-Carbon Combo 對業餘玩家太超規，對 print farm 剛剛好
highlights.body: X1C 約台幣四萬八，P2S Combo 約台幣兩萬六，這個價差怎麼評估，要先問你每天的列印負載是什麼。每週都在跑 PA-CF、PEEK、PA-GF 的話，硬化鋼噴頭跟腔體溫度監測沒得妥協。經營 print farm 的話，雷達首層掃描跟 AI 失敗偵測幾個月內就能用減少廢品回本。但業餘印 PLA 模型的話，X1C 就像開賽車去買菜，買 P2S 把省下來的台幣兩萬一拿去買線材比較實在。

highlights.title: Prusa CORE One 是我唯一信任到 2034 年還能用的機器
highlights.body: Bambu 現在的硬體可靠度很好，但軟體模式是封閉花園，雲端帳號跟自家切片軟體都是必要的。Prusa CORE One 出貨配開源韌體，PrusaSlicer 是消費級切片軟體裡最受尊敬的，Prusa 零件型錄連 2012 年的老機型都還買得到。全鋼外骨骼跟主動腔體 55 度讓這台機器能跑工程塑料到量產品質，MK4S 升級套件讓老 Prusa 用戶可以保留原有工具庫。組裝完成版約台幣三萬九，KIT 版約台幣兩萬六，這就是長期持有的答案。

highlights.title: Elegoo Centauri Carbon 約台幣一萬出頭打破了平價封閉 CoreXY 的天花板
highlights.body: 18 個月前約台幣一萬的封閉式 CoreXY 印表機是不存在的。Centauri Carbon 給你全金屬噴頭、振動補償、Klipper 韌體、封閉腔體，這個價位之前只能買到開放式移動床身機而且速度很差。跟 Bambu 的可靠度差距是真的，軟體生態比較單薄，組裝品質也反映這個價位。想學底層機構的學習者或預算很緊的 maker，這台是台幣六千玩具跟台幣兩萬正經機器之間第一次出現的橋樑。

highlights.title: Ender 3 V3 SE 約台幣七千是真正新手的正確第一台
highlights.body: 推薦新手買台幣兩萬六的 P2S 之前，我都會先問他組過 3D 印表機沒，或者半夜兩點看過列印失敗沒。Ender 3 V3 SE 是學會 3D 列印是什麼的正確方式，台幣七千的移動床身機配 CR Touch 自動調平跟 Sprite 直驅擠出器，讓你犯錯、換噴頭、重組熱端、搞懂為什麼高階 CoreXY 機器值那個錢。三個月後你要嘛確定自己愛上這個興趣升級到 P2S，要嘛二手賣掉半價只虧台幣三千五。這就是進入這個類別風險最低的路徑。

en.commentary: Bambu Lab P2S Combo holds first and Tom's Hardware's dedicated P2S review published this week reaches the same conclusion my own bench testing did: this is the right consumer printer for nine out of ten buyers in 2026. The PMSM servo extruder, the AMS 2 Pro plug-and-play multi-color story, and the invisible bed-leveling combine into the most reliable mid-tier package the category has ever shipped. Bambu Lab X1-Carbon Combo stays at second and the H2C dual-nozzle Vortek demo from Formnext is the signal that Bambu's print-farm story is going to get even more aggressive in H2. Prusa CORE One holds third and the CORE One Plus refresh confirmed this week adds the vent opener and TPU switch upgrades that mid-cycle owners have been asking for, with Bondtech's 8-material module landing later this year. The long-term reliability case for Prusa is still the cleanest in the category and the open ecosystem story matters more for buyers who hate cloud lock-in. Bambu Lab P1S at fourth is being phased out across 2026 and the inventory window for new buyers is closing. Prusa MK4S, Creality K2 Plus Combo, Elegoo Centauri Carbon, and Anycubic Kobra 3 Max Combo are all unchanged. The market story this spring is Bambu consolidating the mainstream while Prusa wins the long-term-reliability buyer; everything else is fighting for the gaps between them.

highlights.title: Tom's Hardware P2S review confirms the consumer-default pick
highlights.body: Tom's Hardware reaches the same conclusion my own bench testing did: P2S Combo is the right printer for nine out of ten buyers in 2026. PMSM servo extruder plus AMS 2 Pro plus invisible bed leveling combine into the most reliable mid-tier package the category has ever shipped.

highlights.title: Prusa CORE One Plus refresh confirms long-term reliability bet
highlights.body: Vent opener and TPU switch upgrades land this cycle, with Bondtech's 8-material module coming later in 2026. The long-term reliability case for Prusa is still the cleanest in the category, and the open ecosystem story matters more for buyers who refuse cloud lock-in.

highlights.title: P1S phase-out shrinks the inventory window for new buyers
highlights.body: The P1S is being phased out across 2026 as P2S takes over the slot. Buyers who specifically want the P1S need to act this quarter or the choice will be made for them by stock-outs. For most buyers the answer is just to step up to the P2S Combo anyway.

zh-tw.commentary:
Bambu Lab P2S Combo 守住第一，Tom's Hardware 這禮拜推出的 P2S 專文評測得到的結論跟我自己工作台測試一樣：2026 年 9 成消費級買家正確的答案就是它。PMSM 伺服馬達擠出器、AMS 2 Pro 即插即用多色、隱形自動調平，這三個組合是這個分類有史以來最可靠的中階包裝。

Bambu Lab X1-Carbon Combo 守第二，Formnext 展上 H2C 雙噴頭 Vortek 系統的展示，是 Bambu print farm 故事下半年會更積極的訊號。

Prusa CORE One 守第三，這禮拜確認的 CORE One Plus 改版加入排氣口控制跟 TPU 切換升級，這些是中期擁有者一直在要的功能，Bondtech 的 8 材料模組今年稍晚會到。Prusa 的長期可靠度論點在這個分類還是最乾淨的，開放生態對討厭雲端綁定的買家更重要。

Bambu Lab P1S 排第四，2026 全年會逐步停產，新買家的庫存窗口正在收。Prusa MK4S、Creality K2 Plus Combo、Elegoo Centauri Carbon、Anycubic Kobra 3 Max Combo 都沒動。

這個春天的市場故事是 Bambu 在收斂主流市場、Prusa 拿下長期可靠度買家，其他人在搶兩家之間的縫隙。台灣這邊三上、3D Mart 的 P2S Combo 出貨開始穩定，PChome 偶爾會看到 P1S 清庫存的限量價，想撿 P1S 的這一兩個月要動作。

highlights.body: Tom's Hardware 得到的結論跟我工作台測試一樣：P2S Combo 是 2026 年 9 成買家正確的答案。PMSM 伺服馬達擠出器加 AMS 2 Pro 加隱形自動調平，是這個分類最可靠的中階包裝。

highlights.title: Prusa CORE One Plus 改版確認長期可靠度的賭注
highlights.body: 排氣口控制跟 TPU 切換升級這一輪到位，Bondtech 的 8 材料模組 2026 年稍晚會到。Prusa 的長期可靠度論點在這個分類還是最乾淨的，開放生態對拒絕雲端綁定的買家更重要。

highlights.title: P1S 停產讓新買家的庫存窗口在收
highlights.body: P1S 2026 全年逐步停產，P2S 接位。特別想要 P1S 的買家這一季要動作，不然會被缺貨幫你做決定。大部分買家答案就是直接升級到 P2S Combo。

en.commentary: I'm slotting the Bambu Lab H2D in at third today. After a week of running it on real engineering jobs the IDEX dual-extruder argument is decisive: the H2D eliminates purge towers entirely on two-material prints, which is the single biggest material-waste fix the category has shipped this year. The 600 mm/s high-flow hotend is genuinely sustained throughput rather than a marketing peak, the BirdsEye top-view camera nails sub-0.3mm alignment, and the AI nozzle camera catches extrusion failures before they ruin a print. At $1,899 base the H2D sits above the X1-Carbon in price and above it in capability for anyone running dual-material engineering work or wanting the optional laser and cutter modules on the same desk. The P2S Combo holds first because the H2D is overspec for the 90% case, and the X1-Carbon stays at second as the print-farm default. Prusa CORE One drops a position to fourth on raw rank shuffle from the H2D insertion, and the CORE One INDX tool-changer kit confirmed this week with Bondtech at €499 for 4 tools and €699 for 8 is the right long-horizon answer for makers who want physical tool swaps rather than filament swaps. Everything below CORE One holds its relative position. The headline for buyers this week: if you've been waiting for true IDEX in a Bambu chassis, the H2D shipped and it earns the price.

highlights.title: Bambu Lab H2D earns third place because IDEX eliminates purge towers entirely
highlights.body: Independent dual extruders on the H2D mean two-material prints run without purge towers. That single architectural choice cuts material waste on dual-color and dual-material jobs to near zero, and the 600 mm/s high-flow hotend turns the time savings into real throughput gains. BirdsEye top-view alignment camera hits sub-0.3mm accuracy and the AI nozzle camera catches extrusion failures live. At $1,899 base the H2D is overspec for hobbyists and exactly right for engineering and creative pros who also want the optional laser and cutter modules on a single desk.

highlights.title: Prusa CORE One INDX confirms the open-source toolchanger answer
highlights.body: Bondtech's CORE One INDX kit lands at €499 for 4 tools and €699 for 8 tools, with physical tool-head swaps mid-print rather than filament swaps. That handles nozzle-size changes, chemically incompatible materials, and even technology switches within one job. Bambu's IDEX wins for two-material throughput, but for makers who want six or eight independent tools, INDX is the open-source path the category has been missing.

highlights.title: P2S Combo still wins for 90% of buyers despite the H2D launch
highlights.body: H2D is the right pick for engineering work, dual-material throughput, and the laser/cutter combo desk. For everyone else the P2S Combo at $799 delivers the reliable mid-tier package that wins on price-performance. The H2D launch shifts the top of the leaderboard but does not move the default recommendation for hobbyists, students, and casual print enthusiasts.

en.commentary: Day three of the Memorial Day push and the 3D printer pricing has barely moved since Friday, which tells me Bambu Lab and Prusa both calibrated their floors carefully this cycle. The P2S Combo is still sitting at $799 on Sunday morning, the same number I saw walking out of the office Friday afternoon, and I am keeping it at the top because nothing in this bracket touches its AMS 2 Pro plus servo extruder combination. The X1-Carbon Combo has not flinched from $1,449 either, and I think Bambu is daring buyers to hold out for a Prime Day discount that I doubt is coming. The H2D at $1,899 stays my pick for makers who want the IDEX dual extruder and the laser module path, even though the value score keeps it third. Prusa CORE One inventory in the US warehouse is visibly thinning today, and the next batch ships in late June at the current $1,199 sticker, so Sunday is the realistic last call. The P1S keeps its fifth slot because at $599 it is still the cleanest entry into the Bambu ecosystem. Lower down, the Centauri Carbon at $299 is the Sunday sleeper I keep recommending to friends who want a Klipper printer with a real chamber. Creality K2 Plus Combo dropped to $1,599 overnight on the official store and that bumps its value score. Tuesday morning resets everything to MSRP, and I expect the P2S to be the first to pop back. If you have been waiting since April, today is the day to click checkout.

en.commentary: Friday and the post-Memorial Day price page has settled. Bambu Lab let the P2S Combo float back to $849 at the official store on Wednesday, which is $50 above the holiday floor but still the strongest mid-range value I track. I keep it at the top because the AMS 2 Pro plus the servo extruder workflow continues to dominate the bracket and the Father's Day landing page Bambu pushed live yesterday is already nudging it back toward $799 for the long weekend three weeks out. The X1-Carbon Combo is parked at $1,499 again, exactly where it sat before the holiday, and that consistency tells me Bambu sees no need to defend further. The H2D at $1,949 stays third for makers who want the IDEX dual extruder and the laser module path, and the new Bambu Studio 2.4 release this week tightened the IDEX calibration noticeably. Prusa CORE One held its $1,199 sticker through the reset and the Hackaday note this morning confirms Prusa caught up on the late-June production batch, so the inventory anxiety I flagged on Sunday has eased. That keeps CORE One the right Father's Day pick for buyers who want all-steel hardware and Czech support. P1S keeps the fifth slot at $649 and remains the cleanest Bambu entry point. Lower down, the Elegoo Centauri Carbon at $329 is still the under-the-radar Father's Day suggestion for Dads who tinker, and the Creality K2 Plus Combo dropped to $1,649 on the official store overnight. Father's Day promos start landing Monday and the next real wave of discounts is mid-June. If you have been waiting, the morning to commit is now before Father's Day demand pulls the value-tier inventory.

references.title: The Best 3D Printers for Home, Workshop or Business in 2026
Best 3D Printers 2026: Our Top Picks in 25 Categories
I reviewed more than 50 of the best 3D printers of 2026

en.commentary: The Bambu Lab P2S Combo holds the top rank in 2026 with a 9.4 overall score, and its case is built on the combination of multi-color capability, print speed, and ecosystem integration that no competitor currently packages as cleanly. The 9.5 scores in print quality, multicolor, and ecosystem reflect a system that delivers professional-quality outputs from a consumer-friendly workflow. The AMS lite multicolor system and automatic calibration mean the P2S Combo is genuinely plug-and-play compared to machines that require tuning expertise. The Bambu Lab X1 Carbon at rank two is the benchmark for print quality with its 9.8 score, but the 7.8 value score reflects its premium pricing relative to the P2S. For users who prioritize absolute output quality over cost, the X1 Carbon is the machine I point to. The Bambu Lab H2D at rank three is the new high-performance entry in the lineup, with 9.7 speeds and 9.7 print quality making it the fastest and most precise consumer machine currently available. Its 7.0 value score marks it as a specialist purchase. The Prusa Core One at rank four is the premium open-source alternative, carrying a 9.8 ecosystem score that reflects Prusa's depth of community support, firmware transparency, and material compatibility. For users who require full control over print parameters and refuse vendor lock-in, the Core One is the answer. The Elegoo Centauri Carbon at rank eight is the budget story of 2026: enclosed CoreXY printing at under $300 with 500mm/s speed capability, delivering capabilities that were $800 machines two years ago.

highlights.title: Bambu Lab P2S Combo Is the Multi-Color Standard
highlights.body: The P2S Combo earns its rank one position through a 9.5 score across print quality, multicolor, and ecosystem. The AMS lite system handles multi-material printing with minimal user intervention, making professional multi-color prints accessible without the tuning expertise that older multi-material systems required.

highlights.title: Elegoo Centauri Carbon Redefines Budget FDM in 2026
highlights.body: Enclosed CoreXY at under $300 with 500mm/s speed capability represents a fundamental shift in what budget 3D printing means. The Centauri Carbon's 9.8 value score is the highest in the category, and it delivers capabilities that were exclusively available in $800+ machines just two years ago.

highlights.title: Prusa Core One Anchors the Open-Source Tier
highlights.body: A 9.8 ecosystem score reflects Prusa's unmatched community documentation, firmware transparency, and material compatibility library. For users who need full parameter control, third-party material support, or refuse closed ecosystem constraints, the Core One is the definitive choice in 2026.

zh-tw.commentary: Bambu Lab P2S Combo 以 9.4 分的總評分守住 2026 年 3D 印表機第一名。多色列印能力、列印速度、生態系整合三個維度各拿 9.5 分，這個組合目前沒有競爭對手能包裝得如此完整。AMS lite 多色系統加上自動校準，讓 P2S Combo 真正做到開箱即用，相比需要大量調整經驗的機器，這個易用性差距在新手和進階用戶身上都很有感。Bambu Lab X1 Carbon 排名第二，9.8 分的列印品質評分是本榜最高，但 7.8 分的性價比反映了它相對 P2S 的溢價。追求絕對輸出品質的用戶，X1 Carbon 是我指向的機器。Bambu Lab H2D 排名第三，9.7 分的速度和 9.7 分的列印品質讓它成為目前最快最精確的消費級機器，7.0 分的性價比定位它為專業用途採購。Prusa Core One 排名第四，9.8 分的生態系評分反映 Prusa 在社群支援深度、韌體透明度和材料相容性的無可匹敵的積累。需要完整參數控制並且拒絕封閉生態系的用戶，Core One 是 2026 年的明確答案。Elegoo Centauri Carbon 是 2026 年的平價傳說，$300 以內的封閉式 CoreXY 機器帶來 500mm/s 的速度，這個規格兩年前要花 $800 以上才買得到，9.8 分的性價比是全榜最高。

highlights.title: Bambu Lab P2S Combo 是多色列印的標準答案
highlights.body: 列印品質、多色能力、生態系三項各拿 9.5 分，讓 P2S Combo 牢牢守住第一名。AMS lite 多色系統最小化用戶干預，讓多色列印從需要專業調整知識的困難任務，變成一般用戶能輕鬆完成的工作流程。

references.title: Bambu Lab teases new A2L 3D printer — June 1 launch hints at a massive, budget-friendly bed slinger
New Bambu Lab X2D 3D printer adds nozzle-switching system
The X1-series is EOL - the standard it set will remain forever

en.commentary: Today is a pivot day for the FDM market and I want my readers to see it coming. Bambu Lab has confirmed a June 1 launch for the A2L, teased as 'a BIG one,' and the 'L' points squarely at a large-format bed slinger meant to replace the aging A1 line. That matters for this ranking because the A1 family was the gateway most beginners used, and a roomier, value-priced successor reshapes the bottom of every buyer's shortlist. At the same time Bambu has formally retired the X1 series as end of life and is steering buyers toward the X2D, which adds a mechanical nozzle-switching system and the AI thermal management borrowed from the H series. I am holding my order steady today because none of these machines have shipped into independent hands yet, and I never reorder on a teaser. My P2S Combo stays at the top: it is the printer I recommend to anyone who wants multicolor done right with the least fuss, and at its current price it is the most complete package you can buy. The X1 Carbon remains my quality benchmark even in EOL status, because the hardware does not get worse the day a press release goes out, and the resale and parts ecosystem is enormous. Prusa's Core One keeps my trust for anyone who values open firmware and serviceability over raw speed. If you have been waiting, the smart move this week is patience. Watch the A2L reviews land, watch X2D street pricing settle, then buy. I will revisit these standings the moment real test prints exist, and I expect movement in the entry tier within a month.

highlights.title: A2L launches June 1
highlights.body: Bambu Lab confirmed a June 1 reveal for the A2L, teased as a large-format bed slinger positioned to succeed the A1 line. It could reshape the value end of this list once units ship.

highlights.title: X1 series is officially EOL
highlights.body: Bambu retired the X1 series as end of life, but the X1 Carbon hardware still prints beautifully and its parts ecosystem is deep, so I keep it at rank 2 today.

highlights.title: X2D brings nozzle switching
highlights.body: The new X2D adds a mechanical nozzle-switching dual-extrusion system plus H-series AI thermal management. Promising, but I wait for independent prints before moving it onto the board.

highlights.title: P2S Combo holds the crown
highlights.body: For hassle-free multicolor at a sane price, the P2S Combo is still the printer I hand to friends. Nothing announced this week changes that recommendation.

highlights.title: My advice: wait a week
highlights.body: With the A2L hours away and X2D pricing unsettled, patience is the value play. I reorder on test prints, never on teasers.

zh-tw.commentary: 今天是 FDM 市場的轉折點，我得先幫大家把風向講清楚。Bambu Lab 已經確認 6 月 1 日要發表 A2L，官方自己預告「這台很大」，名字裡的 L 幾乎就是在講大列印尺寸的床身移動式機型，目標就是接替已經有點年紀的 A1 系列。這對排行榜影響很大，因為 A1 一直是新手入門的第一台，現在出個空間更大又主打性價比的接班人，整個入門價位帶的購買清單都要重新洗牌。同一時間 Bambu 也正式把 X1 系列列為停產 EOL，把買家導向 X2D，這台多了機械式換噴頭系統，還把 H 系列那套 AI 溫控搬過來。老實說我今天不動排名，因為這幾台都還沒到獨立評測者手上，我從不靠一張預告圖就改榜。P2S Combo 繼續坐穩第一，這台就是我會直接推給想玩多色又怕麻煩的人，以現在的售價來看，它是整體最完整的一台。X1 Carbon 就算停產了還是我心中的畫質標竿，發新聞稿那天硬體又不會變差，二手跟零件生態也夠龐大。Prusa Core One 對於看重開源韌體跟好維修的人，依然值得信賴。如果你最近在觀望，這禮拜最聰明的做法就是再等一下。等 A2L 評測出爐，等 X2D 實際街價穩定，再出手。等真正的測試列印件出現，我會立刻重新檢視榜單，我預期一個月內入門級距會有變化。

highlights.title: A2L 6 月 1 日登場
highlights.body: Bambu Lab 確認 6 月 1 日揭曉 A2L，預告是大尺寸床身移動式機型，擺明要接班 A1。等實機鋪貨後，入門價位帶很可能洗牌。

highlights.title: X1 系列正式停產
highlights.body: Bambu 把 X1 系列列為 EOL，但 X1 Carbon 的硬體照樣印得漂亮，零件生態又深，所以我今天還是把它放在第二名。

highlights.title: X2D 帶來機械換噴頭
highlights.body: 新的 X2D 多了機械式換噴頭雙擠出系統，加上 H 系列的 AI 溫控。看起來很香，可是我要等獨立評測列印件才會把它放上榜。

highlights.title: P2S Combo 穩坐第一
highlights.body: 想要省事的多色列印又不想花大錢，P2S Combo 還是我會直接推給朋友的那台。這禮拜的消息都動搖不了它。

highlights.title: 我的建議：再等一週
highlights.body: A2L 幾小時後就揭曉，X2D 價格也還沒穩，這時候耐心才是真正划算的選擇。我只看測試列印件改榜，不看預告圖。

references.title: Bambu Lab Launches A2L: Creative Playground. Extra Large.
Bambu Lab Launches the A2L: A "Creative Playground" with Larger Build Volume and Modular Add-Ons
Bambu Lab teases new A2L 3D printer — June 1 launch hints at a massive, budget-friendly bed slinger

en.commentary:
Bambu Lab just reminded everyone why it owns the top of this list. On June 1 the company launched the A2L, a large-format A-series bed slinger with a 330 x 320 x 325 mm build volume, roughly 105 percent more printing room than the 256 mm-class machines most hobbyists own. It starts at 379 EUR, runs below 49 dB in silent mode, and accepts up to four AMS units for as many as 19 colors in PLA and PETG. That kind of release cadence is exactly why Bambu holds four of our top five spots.

The Bambu Lab P2S Combo stays at rank 1 with a 9.4 overall, and nothing in the A2L launch changes that verdict. The A2L is built for large, family-friendly creative projects at a budget price, while the P2S Combo remains the machine I recommend for anyone who wants the best balance of print quality, speed, and multicolor reliability in a CoreXY frame. Its 9.5 print quality and 9.5 multicolor scores are the reason it beats faster but rougher rivals.

The X1-Carbon at rank 2 is still the workhorse for engineering materials, and the new H2D at rank 3 covers the dual-nozzle, large-format niche that the A2L now complements from below. Prusa holds rank 4 with the Core One, the strongest non-Bambu CoreXY on the market and the pick for buyers who value open ecosystem and repairability.

The practical takeaway: the A2L widens Bambu's lineup at the affordable end without disturbing the premium order. If you want large prints without splitting and gluing, watch the A2L, but the P2S Combo is still the printer to buy for all-around performance heading into summer.

highlights.title: Bambu Lab A2L Launches With Extra-Large Build Volume
highlights.body: Released June 1, the A2L brings a 330 x 320 x 325 mm build volume, 105 percent more print space than standard 256 mm machines, starting at 379 EUR. It runs below 49 dB in silent mode and supports up to four AMS units for 19 colors, widening Bambu's affordable lineup without touching the premium tier.

highlights.title: P2S Combo Stays the All-Around Pick at Rank 1
highlights.body: With 9.5 print quality and 9.5 multicolor scores in a proven CoreXY frame, the Bambu Lab P2S Combo remains the machine I recommend for the best balance of quality, speed, and reliable multicolor printing. The A2L targets large-format creative work, not the all-rounder crown.

highlights.title: Bambu Dominates Four of the Top Five
highlights.body: P2S Combo, X1-Carbon, H2D, and P1S hold four of our five top spots. The relentless release pace, now including the A2L, is why no competitor has matched Bambu's ecosystem depth, with Prusa's Core One the strongest open-ecosystem alternative at rank 4.

zh-tw.commentary:
Bambu Lab 又一次提醒大家為什麼它穩坐這份榜單的頂端。6 月 1 日他們推出了 A2L，一台大尺寸的 A 系列床身移動式印表機，列印空間 330 x 320 x 325 mm，比大多數玩家手上的 256mm 級機型多出大約 105% 的列印體積。起價 379 歐元，靜音模式下噪音低於 49 分貝，還能串接最多四組 AMS，用 PLA 和 PETG 做到 19 色列印。這種出新機的節奏，正是 Bambu 一口氣佔據前五名四席的原因。

第一名的 Bambu Lab P2S Combo 維持 9.4 總分，A2L 的發表完全沒有動搖這個判斷。A2L 走的是大尺寸、親子創作、平價的路線；P2S Combo 則是我推薦給想要列印品質、速度、多色穩定度三者最佳平衡的人，CoreXY 結構加上 9.5 的列印品質和 9.5 的多色分數，這就是它贏過那些跑很快但表面粗糙對手的關鍵。

第二名的 X1-Carbon 還是工程材料的主力機，第三名的新款 H2D 補上雙噴頭大尺寸這塊，而 A2L 從下方把這個區間補得更完整。Prusa 的 Core One 排第四，是市面上最強的非 Bambu CoreXY 機型，重視開放生態和可維修性的人就選它。

講白一點：A2L 在平價端把 Bambu 的產品線拉得更寬，但完全沒有打亂高階機種的順序。想印大件又不想切割黏合，可以盯著 A2L；但論全方位表現，進入夏天該買的還是 P2S Combo。

highlights.title: Bambu Lab A2L 發表，超大列印空間
highlights.body: 6 月 1 日上市的 A2L 提供 330 x 320 x 325 mm 列印空間，比標準 256mm 機型多出 105% 體積，起價 379 歐元。靜音模式噪音低於 49 分貝，可串接四組 AMS 達 19 色，在不動到高階機種的前提下把平價產品線拉寬。

highlights.title: P2S Combo 全方位首選，穩坐第一
highlights.body: 9.5 的列印品質加 9.5 的多色分數，搭配成熟的 CoreXY 結構，Bambu Lab P2S Combo 仍是我推薦給想要品質、速度、多色穩定三者最佳平衡的人。A2L 鎖定大尺寸創作，不是來搶全能王冠的。

highlights.title: Bambu 包辦前五名四席
highlights.body: P2S Combo、X1-Carbon、H2D、P1S 佔據前五名的四席。包括 A2L 在內的密集出機節奏，是沒有對手能追上 Bambu 生態系深度的原因，Prusa Core One 則是第四名最強的開放生態替代選擇。

references.title: Best 3D Printer in 2026: Tested by a 6-Printer Farm Operator
Bambu Lab vs Creality vs Prusa: The Definitive 2026 Comparison
Best 3D Printer 2026: 12 Models Tested and Compared

en.commentary: The Bambu Lab P2S Combo stays my number one, and the reviews this week keep calling it the single best 3D printer you can buy in 2026 for one simple reason, it does everything well and nothing poorly. At $799 you get an enclosed CoreXY frame, 500mm/s print speeds, the AMS 2 Pro included for true four-color work, an upgraded touchscreen, and Bambu's mature software ecosystem. That is a package that just works out of the box, which is exactly what most buyers want. The X1 Carbon holds second as the proven flagship, the printer for people who want the absolute best print quality and LIDAR calibration and do not mind paying for it. The H2D sits third as the large-format speed machine for makers who need a bigger build volume. The Prusa Core One stays fourth, and I want to defend that placement, because Prusa's open-source roots, meticulous engineering, and long-term reliability still mean something for users who value repairability and community over turnkey speed. The news worth flagging this week is Bambu's new A2L, a 330mm budget machine starting at $469 with an optional cutting module, which signals Bambu is now squeezing the affordable end too. It is not a top-three contender yet, but it is a sign of where things are heading. For now, buy the P2S Combo and stop overthinking it.

highlights.title: Bambu Lab P2S Combo is the best all-rounder
highlights.body: Enclosed CoreXY, 500mm/s speeds, included AMS 2 Pro for four-color printing, and a polished software ecosystem at $799 add up to a machine that does everything well. It works straight out of the box, which is why reviewers keep naming it the single best buy of 2026.

highlights.title: Bambu Lab X1 Carbon is the quality flagship
highlights.body: LIDAR calibration and best-in-class print fidelity make it the choice for makers who want flawless results above all. It commands a premium, and for those chasing the cleanest possible prints it earns every dollar.

highlights.title: Prusa Core One is the reliability and openness pick
highlights.body: Built on Prusa's open-source heritage and meticulous engineering, it rewards users who value repairability, documentation, and a deep community. For a printer you intend to run and maintain for years, that foundation matters.

zh-tw.commentary: Bambu Lab P2S Combo 還是我的第一名，這週的評測也一直把它叫成 2026 年最值得買的單一台 3D 印表機，理由很簡單，它什麼都做得好、沒有一項做不好。$799（台幣兩萬四左右）就給你封閉式 CoreXY 結構、500mm/s 列印速度、內附做真四色的 AMS 2 Pro、升級觸控螢幕，還有 Bambu 成熟的軟體生態系。這個組合開箱就能用，正是多數人要的。X1 Carbon 守第二，是經過驗證的旗艦，給想要最頂列印品質跟 LIDAR 校準、又不在意多花錢的人。H2D 排第三，是大尺寸高速機，給需要更大列印空間的玩家。Prusa Core One 維持第四，我想幫這個排名講句話，因為 Prusa 的開源底蘊、精細工程跟長期可靠度，對重視可維修性跟社群勝過開箱即用速度的人來說，還是很有份量。這週值得講的消息是 Bambu 新出的 A2L，330mm 的平價機種，起價 $469（台幣一萬四左右），還有選配的切割模組，代表 Bambu 現在連平價端也在擠壓。它還排不進前三，但這是風向的訊號。現在就買 P2S Combo，別再想太多了。

highlights.title: Bambu Lab P2S Combo 是最強全能機
highlights.body: 封閉式 CoreXY、500mm/s 速度、內附做四色的 AMS 2 Pro，加上成熟軟體生態系，$799 湊成一台什麼都做得好的機子。開箱就能用，這就是評測一直把它選為 2026 最佳單一選擇的原因。

highlights.title: Bambu Lab X1 Carbon 是品質旗艦
highlights.body: LIDAR 校準加頂級列印精度，是把完美成品擺第一的玩家的選擇。它價格偏高，但對追求最乾淨列印的人來說，每一塊錢都值得。

highlights.title: Prusa Core One 是可靠與開放首選
highlights.body: 建立在 Prusa 的開源傳統跟精細工程上，回報那些重視可維修性、文件跟深厚社群的人。要一台打算用好幾年又自己維護的機子，這個底子很重要。

references.title: Bambu Lab P2S review: Refreshing a best seller
The Best 3D Printers for Home, Workshop or Business in 2026

en.commentary: The Bambu Lab P2S Combo holds my number one into June 2026, and Tom's Hardware's review confirms exactly why, it refreshes a best seller without breaking the formula. The P2S keeps everything that made the P1S the default workshop printer and adds a 5 inch color touchscreen, AI powered error detection that catches spaghetti, nozzle clumping and purge jams, a 50C actively regulated chamber, a 300C nozzle, a 70 percent stronger extruder and quieter operation. The decisive part is the price, $549 standalone and $799 for the combo, only $50 more than the machine it replaces, which makes it the best value in serious multicolor printing right now. The X1 Carbon holds second as the proven flagship and the H2D stays third as the dual nozzle workhorse with a bigger build volume, both still worth it if you need their specific strengths, but the P2S simply offers more printer per dollar for most makers. Prusa Core One keeps fourth on its ecosystem and reliability. Nothing this week reorders the leaders, the P2S is the printer I would put in front of almost anyone moving up from a starter machine, and its AMS combo remains the most sensible buy in the lineup.

highlights.title: Bambu Lab P2S refreshes the best seller right
highlights.body: It keeps the P1S formula and adds a 5 inch touchscreen, AI error detection, a 50C regulated chamber, a 300C nozzle and a 70 percent stronger extruder. The upgrades land where they matter for everyday printing.

highlights.title: The price is what seals number one
highlights.body: At $549 standalone and $799 for the combo, it costs only $50 more than the P1S it replaces. That makes it the best value in serious multicolor printing right now.

highlights.title: H2D stays the dual nozzle workhorse
highlights.body: Its bigger build volume, multi material support and dual nozzle setup keep it in third for makers who need that capability. From $1,899 it is the step up when the P2S is not enough.

zh-tw.commentary: Bambu Lab P2S Combo 守住我 2026 年 6 月的第一名，Tom's Hardware 的評測完全印證原因，它把暢銷機改款改得剛剛好，沒有把成功配方搞砸。P2S 保留了讓 P1S 成為工作室預設機的一切，再加上 5 吋彩色觸控螢幕、AI 錯誤偵測能抓義大利麵、噴嘴結塊跟換料堵塞、50 度主動控溫腔體、300 度噴嘴、強化 70% 的擠出機，運作還更安靜。決定性的是價格，單機 549 美元、combo 799 美元，只比它取代的機型貴 50 美元，這讓它成為現在認真做多色列印裡性價比最高的選擇。X1 Carbon 守第二，是久經考驗的旗艦，H2D 守第三，是大列印空間的雙噴嘴主力機，兩台只要你需要它們的特長都還是值得，但對大多數玩家來說，P2S 每塊錢買到的就是更多。Prusa Core One 守第四，靠生態系跟可靠度。這週沒有消息動到前段班，P2S 是我幾乎會推薦給任何從入門機升級的人的機型，它的 AMS combo 還是整個產品線裡最合理的買法。

highlights.title: Bambu Lab P2S 把暢銷機改款改得對
highlights.body: 保留 P1S 配方，加上 5 吋觸控螢幕、AI 錯誤偵測、50 度控溫腔體、300 度噴嘴跟強化 70% 的擠出機。升級都加在日常列印真正用得到的地方。

highlights.title: 價格是封王關鍵
highlights.body: 單機 549 美元、combo 799 美元，只比它取代的 P1S 貴 50 美元。這讓它成為現在認真做多色列印裡性價比最高的選擇。

highlights.title: H2D 還是雙噴嘴主力機
highlights.body: 更大的列印空間、多材料支援跟雙噴嘴設計，讓它守住第三，給需要這些能力的玩家。從 1,899 美元起，是 P2S 不夠用時的升級選擇。

references.title: Best 3D Printer 2026: 12 Models Tested and Compared
Bambu Lab vs Creality vs Prusa: The Definitive 2026 Comparison
Bambu vs Prusa vs Creality 2026 — Full Comparison Guide

en.commentary: Heading into June 2026 the 3D-printer hierarchy holds, and the Bambu Lab P2S Combo stays at number one because it is simply the single best printer you can buy right now. At around 799 dollars it bundles an enclosed CoreXY, 500mm/s speeds, the AMS 2 Pro for clean four-color printing, an upgraded touchscreen and the best software ecosystem in consumer 3D printing. For the overwhelming majority of buyers, this is the answer, and nothing this month changes that. The fresh news is at the entry end: Bambu launched the A2L in June, a 330mm budget machine from 469 dollars with an optional cutting module, which is a great new on-ramp but slots below the flagship combos rather than challenging them. The X1-Carbon Combo holds second as the no-compromise flagship for those who want the absolute best print quality and do not blink at the price. The H2D stays third as the large-format, dual-nozzle powerhouse for makers who need the build volume and versatility. Prusa's CORE One keeps fourth as the best non-Bambu choice, the pick for open-source loyalists who value repairability and Prusa's legendary support, even at 1,199 assembled. The P1S holds fifth and remains my value-king recommendation for a first serious printer. Down the board Creality's K2 Plus, Elegoo's Centauri Carbon and the budget Ender 3 V3 SE carry forward. My read: P2S Combo for almost everyone, X1C for the perfectionist, Prusa for the tinkerer, P1S or the new A2L if money is tight.

highlights.title: P2S Combo is the single best buy
highlights.body: Around 799 dollars gets enclosed CoreXY, 500mm/s, AMS 2 Pro four-color printing and the best software in consumer 3D printing. For the overwhelming majority of buyers it is the answer, so it stays number one.

highlights.title: Bambu's new A2L widens the on-ramp
highlights.body: The June launch is a 330mm budget machine from 469 dollars with an optional cutting module. It is a great entry point, but it slots below the flagship combos rather than challenging the top of the board.

highlights.title: X1-Carbon is the perfectionist's flagship
highlights.body: For buyers who want the absolute best print quality and do not blink at the price, it remains the no-compromise pick. That ceiling keeps it a deserved second behind the better-value P2S.

highlights.title: Prusa CORE One is the best non-Bambu pick
highlights.body: Enclosed CoreXY, 500mm/s and legendary repairability and support make it the choice for open-source loyalists. Even at 1,199 assembled it earns its fourth spot for people who value owning every part.

zh-tw.commentary: 2026 年 6 月，3D 列印機的階層守住，Bambu Lab P2S Combo 還是第一，因為它就是你現在能買到最好的單一機型。台幣大約兩萬五出頭，它就把封閉式 CoreXY、500mm/s 速度、能乾淨四色列印的 AMS 2 Pro、升級觸控螢幕、加上消費級 3D 列印裡最好的軟體生態，全部打包進去。對絕大多數買家，答案就是它，這個月沒有任何事改變這點。新消息在入門端，Bambu 6 月推出 A2L，330mm 的平價機種，台幣約一萬五起，可選切割模組，是很棒的新入門管道，但它落在旗艦 Combo 之下，而不是來挑戰它們。X1-Carbon Combo 守第二，是不妥協的旗艦，給想要絕對最佳列印品質、又不眨眼看價格的人。H2D 守第三，是大尺寸雙噴頭怪獸，給需要列印體積跟多功能性的玩家。Prusa 的 CORE One 守第四，是非 Bambu 裡最好的選擇，給重視可維修性跟 Prusa 傳奇級支援的開源死忠，就算組裝好要台幣三萬八也值得。P1S 守第五，依然是我推薦的第一台認真機種的性價比王。再往下，Creality K2 Plus、Elegoo Centauri Carbon、平價的 Ender 3 V3 SE 照舊。我的看法是，幾乎所有人選 P2S Combo，完美主義者選 X1C，愛動手的選 Prusa，預算緊就選 P1S 或新的 A2L。

highlights.title: P2S Combo 是最強單一選擇
highlights.body: 台幣約兩萬五出頭就有封閉 CoreXY、500mm/s、AMS 2 Pro 四色列印、加上消費級最好的軟體。對絕大多數買家就是答案，所以第一還是它。

highlights.title: Bambu 新 A2L 拓寬入門管道
highlights.body: 6 月推出的 330mm 平價機種，台幣約一萬五起，可選切割模組。是很棒的入門點，但它落在旗艦 Combo 之下，不是來挑戰榜首的。

highlights.title: X1-Carbon 是完美主義者旗艦
highlights.body: 想要絕對最佳列印品質、又不眨眼看價格的買家，它還是不妥協的選擇。這個天花板讓它穩居性價比更好的 P2S 之後、拿下第二。

The Best 3D Printers of 2026

en.commentary: The Bambu Lab P2S Combo stays at number one because Bambu Lab has done to 3D printing what Apple did to phones: it made the technology just work. The P2S prints fast, prints clean out of the box with almost no tuning, and the AMS multicolor system makes multi-material prints genuinely accessible rather than a fight. For most people, whether a beginner or an experienced maker who values their time, this is the printer that delivers results without the traditional 3D-printing frustration. The Bambu Lab X1-Carbon holds second as the flagship for people who want the absolute best print quality and the full feature set. The Bambu Lab H2D stays third as the large-format option for bigger prints. The Prusa Core One holds fourth as the open-ecosystem alternative for people who want to tinker and own their machine fully. Nothing this week reordered the field. The read: best all-round is the P2S Combo, best quality is the X1-Carbon, best open-source is the Prusa Core One. Bambu won on ease, but Prusa still wins for people who value repairability and an open ecosystem over plug-and-play convenience.

highlights.title: Bambu Lab P2S Combo makes 3D printing just work
highlights.body: It prints fast and clean out of the box with almost no tuning, and the AMS makes multicolor genuinely accessible. For beginners and time-pressed makers alike, it delivers results without frustration at number one.

highlights.title: Bambu Lab X1-Carbon is the quality flagship
highlights.body: The absolute best print quality and the full feature set make it the pick for people who want the ceiling. For makers who prioritize output quality, it holds a firm second.

highlights.title: Prusa Core One is the open-ecosystem pick
highlights.body: It is the alternative for people who want to tinker, repair and fully own their machine. For makers who value openness over plug-and-play, it holds a solid fourth on principle and repairability.

highlights.title: Ease versus openness is the real choice
highlights.body: Bambu won on plug-and-play convenience; Prusa still wins for repairability and an open ecosystem. Decide whether you want results immediately or a machine you can fully control, and the pick follows.

zh-tw.commentary: Bambu Lab P2S Combo 我還是放第一，因為 Bambu Lab 對 3D 列印做的事，就像 Apple 對手機做的：它讓這項技術就這樣能用。P2S 列印快、開箱幾乎不用調就印得乾淨，AMS 多色系統讓多材料列印真的變得親民、而非一場仗。對多數人，無論是新手還是珍惜時間的老手，這台印表機在沒有傳統 3D 列印挫折下交出成果。Bambu Lab X1-Carbon 守第二，是旗艦，給想要絕對最佳列印品質跟完整功能的人。Bambu Lab H2D 守第三，是大尺寸選擇，印更大的東西。Prusa Core One 守第四，是開放生態替代，給想要折騰、完全擁有自己機器的人。這週沒事件重排。判斷：全能選 P2S Combo、品質選 X1-Carbon、開源選 Prusa Core One。Bambu 靠易用贏了，但 Prusa 依然贏得那些重視可維修跟開放生態勝過隨插即用的人。

highlights.title: Bambu Lab P2S Combo 讓 3D 列印就這樣能用
highlights.body: 開箱幾乎不用調就印得又快又乾淨，AMS 讓多色真的親民。對新手跟時間緊的老手，它在第一名沒有挫折地交出成果。

highlights.body: 絕對最佳的列印品質跟完整功能，讓它是想要天花板的人的選擇。重視成品品質的玩家，它穩在第二。

highlights.title: Prusa Core One 是開放生態選擇
highlights.body: 它是給想要折騰、維修、完全擁有自己機器的人的替代。重視開放勝過隨插即用的玩家，它靠原則跟可維修穩坐第四。

highlights.title: 易用對開放是真正的選擇
highlights.body: Bambu 靠隨插即用便利贏了；Prusa 依然贏在可維修跟開放生態。決定你要立刻有成果還是一台能完全掌控的機器，選擇就跟著出來。

en.commentary:
The Bambu Lab P2S Combo stays first, and a fresh round of 2026 head-to-head testing from a six-printer farm operator lands on the same verdict my bench has held for weeks: at $799 this is the right printer for nine out of ten buyers. The enclosed CoreXY chassis, the PMSM servo extruder with roughly 70% more grip than the P1S, AMS 2 Pro four-color drying, and 500 mm/s speeds combine into the most reliable mid-tier package the category has ever shipped. Out of the box it produces results that used to take weeks of manual calibration, and after a month of mixed PLA, PETG, and ASA my unit still has not failed a multi-day print.

The X1-Carbon Combo holds second at $1,449 as the print-farm and engineering default. The hardened steel hotend is the only correct answer for abrasive composites, and the lidar first-layer scan plus AI failure detection pay back in scrap reduction at scale. The H2D stays third because its IDEX dual extruders eliminate purge towers entirely on two-material jobs, which is the biggest material-waste fix the category shipped this year.

Prusa CORE One holds fourth and remains the cleanest long-term-reliability bet in the market. The all-steel exoskeleton, active 55C chamber, open firmware, and a spare-parts catalog that still covers machines from 2012 make this the printer that will still work in 2034. The trade-off is multi-color: the MMU is fiddlier than AMS, and that gap keeps it behind the Bambu trio.

The P1S at $399 stays fifth and remains the highest-value entry into the Bambu ecosystem, the printer I hand to anyone testing whether they actually want to print. Below it the order holds: MK4S for open-source precision, K2 Plus for large format, Centauri Carbon as the budget enclosed CoreXY that broke the price barrier, and the Ender 3 V3 SE at $218 as the lowest-risk first printer. The spring story is unchanged. Bambu owns the mainstream, Prusa owns the long-horizon buyer, and everyone else fights for the gaps.

highlights.title: The Bambu P2S Combo is still the right answer for 90% of buyers
highlights.body: A six-printer farm operator's 2026 testing reaches the same conclusion my bench has: at $799 the P2S Combo is the default. The PMSM servo extruder delivers 70% more grip than the P1S and fixes the under-extrusion that haunted engineering filaments at speed. AMS 2 Pro handles four-color drying, the enclosed CoreXY runs 500 mm/s, and out of the box it produces results that used to demand weeks of calibration. A month of daily mixed-material printing, zero failed multi-day jobs.

highlights.title: The X1-Carbon Combo is the print-farm and composites default
highlights.body: At $1,449 the X1C earns its premium for the buyer running PA-CF, PEEK, or abrasive composites weekly. The hardened steel hotend is the only correct answer for those materials, and the lidar first-layer scan plus AI failure detection pay back in scrap reduction the moment you run a farm. For a hobbyist on PLA it is overspec, and the P2S delivers most of the result for far less.

highlights.title: Prusa CORE One is the only printer I trust to still run in 2034
highlights.body: The all-steel exoskeleton, active 55C chamber, open firmware, and PrusaSlicer make this the long-horizon answer. Prusa's spare-parts catalog still covers machines from 2012, and the open ecosystem matters more for buyers who refuse cloud lock-in. The MMU is fiddlier than Bambu's AMS, and that multi-color gap is the only reason it sits behind the Bambu trio rather than ahead.

highlights.title: The H2D earns third because IDEX eliminates purge towers entirely
highlights.body: Independent dual extruders mean two-material prints run with zero purge towers, the single biggest material-waste fix the category shipped this year. The 600 mm/s high-flow hotend turns that into real throughput, and the optional laser and cutter modules put 3D printing, engraving, and cutting on one desk. At $1,899 it is overspec for hobbyists and exactly right for dual-material engineering work.

zh-tw.commentary:
Bambu Lab P2S Combo 守住第一，這陣子一個經營六台機器 print farm 的玩家做了 2026 新一輪對決測試，結論跟我工作台這幾週一樣，講白了約台幣兩萬六這台就是九成買家的正確答案。封閉式 CoreXY 機殼、比 P1S 抓力多 70% 的 PMSM 伺服馬達擠出器、AMS 2 Pro 四色乾燥、500 mm/s 速度，組合起來是這個品類有史以來最可靠的中階包裝。開箱即用就能印出過去要手動校正好幾週才有的結果，我這個月混 PLA、PETG、ASA 連續操機，到現在一次多日列印失敗都沒有。

X1-Carbon Combo 約台幣四萬八守第二，是 print farm 跟工程用途的預設。硬化鋼噴頭是耐磨複合材料的唯一正解，雷達首層掃描加 AI 失敗偵測在量產規模下靠減少廢品回本。H2D 守第三，因為 IDEX 雙獨立擠出器在雙材料列印時完全不用 purge tower，這是今年品類最大的材料節省改進。

Prusa CORE One 守第四，還是市場上長期可靠度最乾淨的賭注。全鋼外骨骼、主動腔體加熱 55 度、開源韌體，加上零件型錄連 2012 年的老機型都還買得到，這台到 2034 年都還會運作。要妥協的是多色，MMU 比 AMS 麻煩，這個差距讓它排在 Bambu 三台之後。

P1S 約台幣一萬三守第五，還是進 Bambu 生態系 CP 值最高的入門，我都把這台推給還在猶豫要不要玩的人。下面排序不變，MK4S 走開源精度路線、K2 Plus 走大尺寸、Centauri Carbon 是打破價格天花板的平價封閉 CoreXY，Ender 3 V3 SE 約台幣七千是風險最低的第一台。這個春天的故事沒變，Bambu 收主流、Prusa 拿長期持有派，其他人在搶縫隙。台灣這邊三上、3D Mart 的 P2S Combo 出貨穩定，想入手的隨時都買得到。

highlights.title: Bambu P2S Combo 還是九成買家的正確答案
highlights.body: 一個經營六台機器 print farm 的玩家 2026 測試結論跟我工作台一樣，約台幣兩萬六的 P2S Combo 就是預設。PMSM 伺服馬達擠出器比 P1S 抓力多 70%，修掉高速印工程塑料的出料不穩。AMS 2 Pro 顧四色乾燥，封閉 CoreXY 跑 500 mm/s，開箱即用就有過去要校正好幾週的結果。整個月每天混料列印，零多日失敗。

highlights.title: X1-Carbon Combo 是 print farm 跟複合材料的預設
highlights.body: 約台幣四萬八的 X1C，對每週跑 PA-CF、PEEK、耐磨複合材料的人值這個溢價。硬化鋼噴頭是這些材料的唯一正解，雷達首層掃描加 AI 失敗偵測一經營 farm 就靠減少廢品回本。業餘印 PLA 的話太超規，P2S 用少很多的錢給你大部分結果。

highlights.title: Prusa CORE One 是我唯一信任到 2034 年還能跑的機器
highlights.body: 全鋼外骨骼、主動腔體 55 度、開源韌體、PrusaSlicer，這台是長期持有的答案。Prusa 零件型錄連 2012 年老機型都還買得到，開放生態對拒絕雲端綁定的買家更重要。MMU 比 Bambu AMS 麻煩，這個多色差距是它排在 Bambu 三台之後的唯一原因。

highlights.title: H2D 拿第三，因為 IDEX 直接消滅 purge tower
highlights.body: 兩支獨立擠出器讓雙材料列印零 purge tower，是今年品類最大的材料節省改進。600 mm/s 高流量噴頭把它變成真吞吐量，選配雷射跟切割模組讓 3D 列印、雕刻、切割共用一張桌。約台幣六萬三對業餘玩家太超規，對雙材料工程件剛剛好。

en.commentary: My bench position holds steady this week, and the news cycle backs it up. Bambu Lab launched the larger A2L in June, a 330mm budget machine starting around $469 with an optional cutting module, but that is a beginner and craft play that sits below my current top eleven on raw capability. It does not move the P2S Combo, which at $799 still gives you an enclosed CoreXY, 500mm/s motion, and a four-color AMS 2 Pro in one box. A six-printer farm operator landed on the same conclusion in fresh testing this month, which is exactly the kind of real-world endorsement I trust more than a spec sheet. The X1 Carbon and H2D keep their spots for buyers who want lidar quality control or the big dual-nozzle envelope. On the Prusa side, the CORE One remains the strongest answer to Bambu for people who value serviceability and open hardware, and chatter about a CORE One L for large format is worth watching but is not shipping in volume yet. Creality’s K2 Plus Combo and Elegoo’s Centauri Carbon continue to deliver real value lower down the list. Nothing this week changes the core math: Bambu owns the convenience tier, Prusa owns the tinkerer tier, and the budget machines are getting genuinely good. I am carrying every rank and score forward.

highlights.title: The Bambu P2S Combo is still the buy for most people
highlights.body: At $799 you get an enclosed CoreXY, 500mm/s speeds, and a four-color AMS 2 Pro in the box. A six-printer farm operator reached the same verdict in this month’s testing, and that hands-on endorsement is why it keeps the top spot.

highlights.title: Bambu’s new A2L is a budget and craft machine, not a top-eleven threat
highlights.body: The June A2L starts around $469 with a 330mm bed and an optional cutting module. It is a strong entry point for beginners and makers, but on raw capability it sits below the enclosed CoreXY machines that lead this list.

highlights.title: Prusa CORE One owns the serviceability tier
highlights.body: For buyers who value open hardware and field repair over plug-and-play, the CORE One at $1,199 assembled remains Prusa’s best printer and the most credible alternative to Bambu. A large-format CORE One L is rumored and worth tracking.

zh-tw.commentary: 這週榜單我維持原樣，新聞也支持這個判斷。Bambu 六月推出比較大台的 A2L，330mm 列印範圍，起價大約美金 469 元，還可以加購切割模組，不過老實說這是給新手跟手作族的入門機，純論能力還排不進我目前的前十一名。它撼動不了 P2S Combo。這台 $799 一次給你封閉式 CoreXY、500mm/s 速度，加上四色 AMS 2 Pro，全部包好。這個月有位同時操作六台機器的列印場主在實測後也得到一樣的結論，這種第一線的肯定我比規格表更信。X1 Carbon 跟 H2D 也守住位置，前者有 lidar 品管，後者有雙噴頭大空間。Prusa 這邊，CORE One 仍然是最能跟 Bambu 抗衡的選擇，特別適合重視可維修跟開放硬體的人，外傳有大尺寸的 CORE One L 值得留意，但還沒大量出貨。Creality K2 Plus Combo 跟 Elegoo Centauri Carbon 在中段繼續提供實在的性價比。這週沒有任何事件改變核心邏輯，所以分數跟名次我全部沿用。

highlights.title: Bambu P2S Combo 對多數人仍是最該買的那台
highlights.body: $799 給你封閉式 CoreXY、500mm/s 速度，還附四色 AMS 2 Pro。這個月一位操作六台機器的列印場主實測後也是同一個結論，這種實戰肯定就是它穩坐第一的原因。

highlights.title: 新的 A2L 是入門手作機，不是前段威脅
highlights.body: 六月推出的 A2L 約台幣感受上是輕鬆入手的價位，起價美金 469 元，330mm 床身還能加切割模組。給新手跟手作族很適合，但純論能力，它排在榜單前段那些封閉式 CoreXY 機器後面。

highlights.title: Prusa CORE One 穩坐可維修這一塊
highlights.body: 如果你重視開放硬體跟自己修的能力，勝過開箱即用，組裝完成版 $1,199 的 CORE One 仍是 Prusa 最強的機器，也是最有說服力的 Bambu 替代方案。傳聞中的大尺寸 CORE One L 值得追蹤。

en.commentary: The P2S Combo is still the printer I hand to anyone who asks me where to start in 2026, and nothing this week changed that. For around $799 you get an enclosed CoreXY machine that hits 500mm/s, four-color printing through AMS 2 Pro, and the cleanest software pipeline in the hobby. That combination is why it stays at the top. The X1 Carbon remains the choice when print quality is the single thing you care about, since its hardened toolhead and lidar flow calibration still produce the sharpest walls I have measured. What I keep coming back to is how settled the Bambu ecosystem feels now. Prints just work, slicing is fast, and the AMS handles spools without babysitting. Prusa earns its position differently. The Core One is the first Prusa that matches Bambu on speed while keeping the open platform and the support reputation that has carried Prusa for a decade, and a sub 2C bed variance across a 300mm plate is a real number I trust for large flat parts. Further down, the Elegoo Centauri Carbon stays the value shock of the list, delivering enclosed CoreXY performance for far less money than anyone expected a year ago. The Ender 3 V3 SE is still where I send people on the tightest budget who want to actually learn the craft. This is a mature market, and stability at the top reflects that maturity rather than a lack of progress.

highlights.title: P2S Combo is the safe default
highlights.body: At roughly $799 the enclosed CoreXY, 500mm/s speed, and AMS 2 four-color printing give you the most capability per dollar, which is why I keep it at number one.

highlights.title: X1 Carbon for pure quality
highlights.body: The hardened toolhead and lidar calibration still produce the sharpest walls and most reliable first layers I have measured this year.

highlights.title: Prusa Core One holds its ground
highlights.body: It finally matches Bambu speeds while keeping the open ecosystem and sub 2C bed uniformity across a 300mm plate, ideal for large flat prints.

highlights.title: Elegoo Centauri Carbon is the value pick
highlights.body: Enclosed CoreXY performance at a price that undercuts the field makes it the easiest recommendation for budget buyers who still want speed.

highlights.title: Ender 3 V3 SE to learn on
highlights.body: For the tightest budgets it remains the printer I suggest when someone wants to understand the craft before spending more.

zh-tw.commentary: 老實說，2026 年到現在，有人問我新手該買哪台，我還是直接講 P2S Combo。台幣大概兩萬五出頭，你就拿到封閉式 CoreXY、500mm/s 速度、AMS 2 Pro 四色列印，加上整個圈子裡最順的軟體流程。這個組合就是它穩坐第一的原因。X1 Carbon 我會留給只在乎列印品質的人，它的硬化噴頭加雷達流量校正，做出來的牆面是我今年量過最銳利的。講白了，現在 Bambu 整個生態系真的很成熟，丟進去就印，切片快，換料不用一直顧。Prusa 的價值是另一種。Core One 是第一台速度追上 Bambu 的 Prusa，同時保住開放平台跟那個撐了十年的售後口碑，300mm 熱床溫差壓在 2 度以內，印大片平面我很放心。再往下看，Elegoo Centauri Carbon 還是這張榜的價格震撼彈，用遠低於一年前想像的金額，就給你封閉式 CoreXY 的水準。預算真的很緊又想認真學的人，我還是推 Ender 3 V3 SE。說真的，這個市場已經很成熟了，榜單頂端這麼穩，反映的是成熟，不是沒進步。

highlights.title: P2S Combo 是最穩的預設值
highlights.body: 台幣兩萬五左右就有封閉式 CoreXY、500mm/s 跟 AMS 2 四色列印，每一塊錢換到的功能最多，所以我擺第一。

highlights.title: X1 Carbon 衝品質
highlights.body: 硬化噴頭配雷達校正，牆面銳利度跟首層穩定度是我今年量過最好的，純拚品質就選它。

highlights.title: Prusa Core One 守住陣地
highlights.body: 速度終於追上 Bambu，又保有開放生態系，300mm 熱床溫差壓在 2 度內，印大片平面特別放心。

highlights.title: Elegoo Centauri Carbon 主打俗擱大碗
highlights.body: 用壓過全場的價格給你封閉式 CoreXY 水準，預算有限又想要速度的人最好推。

highlights.title: Ender 3 V3 SE 拿來練手
highlights.body: 預算抓很緊又想先把基本功摸熟的人，我還是推這台入門。

Value hunters still have strong options lower down. Nothing this week reshuffles the order, but the A2L launch makes Bambu's grip on the budget-to-midrange band feel firmer than ever.

The Prusa Core One remains my pick for tinkerers who value open documentation and a repair-anything ethos. This is a mature market right now, and that stability is good news for buyers who can shop on price and features rather than chasing the newest release.

It widens the entry lineup rather than replacing the P2S, so my ranking holds, though I am watching how the A2L lands with buyers who want large-format on a tight budget. The X1-Carbon stays second on the strength of its enclosed chamber and hardened toolpath for carbon-filled filaments. The H2D remains the throughput champion for people running a small farm, and the print speed there is genuinely impressive once you tune it. Prusa keeps the CORE One in fourth because the open firmware and the CORE One L large-format option give engineers room to tinker that the Bambu ecosystem deliberately closes off. Down the value tier, the Elegoo Centauri Carbon continues to punch above its price, and the Ender 3 V3 SE is still the honest budget pick for a first machine. I made no rank changes this cycle. The market moved sideways, with new launches expanding choice instead of reshuffling the leaders.

highlights.title: Bambu A2L expands the budget tier

highlights.title: P2S Combo is still the safe pick
highlights.body: At roughly $549 the P2S Combo pairs quick core-XY motion with the AMS multicolor system. It is the machine I recommend to anyone who values reliable prints over tinkering, which is why it holds the top spot.

highlights.title: Prusa keeps the open-source lane
highlights.body: The CORE One and the larger CORE One L give engineers editable firmware and deep customization. That openness is the reason Prusa stays in the top four even as Bambu dominates on convenience.

highlights.body: The Elegoo Centauri Carbon delivers carbon-capable printing at a budget price, and the Ender 3 V3 SE remains a sensible first printer. Both keep the lower ranks worth a look for cost-focused buyers.

en.commentary: The Bambu Lab P2S Combo stays my top pick this week and the reason is simple. It packs the AI monitoring, the DynaSense extruder with grinding detection, and the full AMS multi-color workflow into a machine that lands at a price most makers can actually justify. I keep coming back to it because it just prints, hour after hour, with almost no babysitting. The X1 Carbon holds second on raw print quality and its rock solid AMS ecosystem, and the H2D remains the pick when you want the widest material range and dual-nozzle throughput in one body. What is worth flagging this week is the H2C reveal at Formnext. Bambu Lab showed a dual-nozzle machine with a Vortek nozzle swapper that finally kills the purge waste that has followed the AMS system since the X1 Carbon launched. That is a real signal about where the roadmap is heading, and I expect it to reshape the top of this list once units ship and we test them. For now I am holding every rank steady because nothing on shelves has changed. Prusa continues to own the open ecosystem and repairability crown with the Core One and MK4S, and I still recommend the P1S as the smartest value enclosed printer for anyone printing ABS or ASA on a budget. The Elegoo Centauri Carbon and Sovol SV08 remain the value plays worth watching for builders who like to tinker.

en.commentary: I keep the Bambu Lab P2S Combo at the top this week because nothing shipping right now beats its balance of a $799 price, an enclosed CoreXY frame, 500mm/s speeds, and a bundled AMS 2 Pro for clean four-color work. The bigger story is Formnext, where Bambu Lab and Prusa both showed next-generation hardware. The Bambu Lab H2C dual-nozzle pairs a Vortek nozzle system that eliminates the usual purge waste, which is a real advance for anyone printing multi-material overnight. That hardware is a preview, so I am holding today's ranks steady until units reach reviewers. For most buyers the decision still lands on the P2S Combo for turnkey reliability, the X1 Carbon when print quality is the priority, and the Prusa Core One for people who value the open ecosystem and long-term serviceability. The P1S remains my value champion at rank 5 because it delivers Bambu quality for the lowest real-world cost. Budget hunters get genuine performance from the Elegoo Centauri Carbon and the Sovol SV08. I run these machines daily, and the pattern holds: Bambu wins on ease, Prusa wins on trust, and the gap between them narrows every quarter.

highlights.title: P2S Combo stays the default pick
highlights.body: At $799 with an enclosed CoreXY design, 500mm/s speeds, and AMS 2 Pro included, it remains the printer I hand to anyone who wants strong results out of the box.

highlights.title: Formnext points to a dual-nozzle future
highlights.body: The Bambu Lab H2C uses a Vortek nozzle to remove purge waste on multi-material prints, which matters most for long unattended jobs.

highlights.title: Prusa Core One holds the ecosystem lead
highlights.body: The enclosed CoreXY body at $1,199 assembled pairs with Prusa's open software and parts supply, ideal for makers who repair and upgrade over years.

highlights.title: Value tier keeps getting stronger
highlights.body: The Elegoo Centauri Carbon and Sovol SV08 deliver reliable speed and quality at prices that make a first serious printer easy to justify.

zh-tw.commentary: 這週我還是把 Bambu Lab P2S Combo 放在第一，理由很直接：現在買得到的機器裡，沒有誰能同時給你約台幣兩萬四的價格、封閉式 CoreXY 結構、500mm/s 速度，再附一組 AMS 2 Pro 做乾淨的四色列印。這禮拜真正的大新聞在 Formnext，Bambu Lab 跟 Prusa 都秀出了下一代機種。Bambu Lab H2C 雙噴頭搭配 Vortek 噴嘴系統，把多材料列印時的廢料清料問題直接解掉，對於半夜掛機跑多材料的人來說是實打實的進步。不過那是預覽性質，實機還沒到評測手上，所以我今天先按兵不動。對大多數人來說，選擇依然很清楚：想開箱即用就選 P2S Combo，追列印品質就上 X1 Carbon，重視開放生態系跟長期維修就挑 Prusa Core One。第五名的 P1S 一直是我心中的性價比王，用 Bambu 的品質換到最低的實際花費。預算有限的話，Elegoo Centauri Carbon 跟 Sovol SV08 給的性能是真的夠用。我天天在跑這些機器，趨勢很明顯：Bambu 贏在好上手，Prusa 贏在信任度，兩邊的差距每一季都在縮小。

highlights.title: P2S Combo 依舊是首選
highlights.body: 約台幣兩萬四、封閉 CoreXY、500mm/s，還附 AMS 2 Pro，我要推薦給想直接印出好成品的人，還是它。

highlights.title: Formnext 指向雙噴頭時代
highlights.body: Bambu Lab H2C 用 Vortek 噴嘴解決多材料列印的清料浪費，對長時間掛機的人特別有感。

highlights.title: Prusa Core One 穩住生態系優勢
highlights.body: 組裝好約台幣三萬六，封閉 CoreXY 機身配上 Prusa 的開放軟體跟零件供應，適合會自己修、想用好幾年的玩家。

highlights.title: 平價層級越來越強
highlights.body: Elegoo Centauri Carbon 跟 Sovol SV08 用讓人下得了手的價格，給出可靠的速度跟品質，第一台認真的機器很好選。
